chunk_text: عن حُمْرانَ مَوْلَى عُثْمَانَ بْنِ عَفَانَ
why_this_chunk_boundary_is_plausible: The owner explicitly gives this as a natural stopping point for explanation about who حمران was.
how_scholar_disagreement_on_chunking_sho: Use scholar chunking as inspiration, then normalize by reasoning toward a manageable canonical chunk.
main_complexity_risk: Different scholars may split this into smaller pieces, causing chunk explosion if copied literally.
preferred_resolution_direction: Prefer one reasonable canonical chunk that can absorb narrower scholar comments.

why_this_chunk_boundary_is_plausible: The owner explicitly points to this as a second explanatory stop where the meaning of 'وَضوء' becomes relevant.
how_scholar_disagreement_on_chunking_sho: Allow smaller scholar-specific remarks to land under the broader canonical chunk when safe.
main_complexity_risk: Overfitting to scholar chunking may create too many tiny branches.
preferred_resolution_direction: Use chunking inspiration, not chunking copy.

chunk_text: Whole hadith family beneath one raw hadith node with optional chunk sub-branches
how_scholar_disagreement_on_chunking_sho: Normalize across scholars instead of reproducing every divergence.
main_complexity_risk: Long hadiths and many scholars create incompatible pause patterns.
preferred_resolution_direction: Make an intelligent chunking decision point rather than blindly following any single scholar.

chunk_text: General principle rather than a single chunk
why_this_chunk_boundary_is_plausible: The owner explicitly rejects trying to please every scholar’s chunking choice exactly.
how_scholar_disagreement_on_chunking_sho: Treat scholar chunking as evidence, not binding schema.
main_complexity_risk: Literal copying leads to branch inflation and study confusion.
preferred_resolution_direction: Reasoned canonical chunking with preserved relation to scholar variations.
